title: galium aparine var. infestum
intro: Galium aparine var. infestum, commonly known as stickyweed or cleavers, is a variant of the Galium aparine plant, characterized by its sticky stems and leaves that cling to surfaces. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Galium aparine var. infestum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system, as well as potentially aiding in the treatment of conditions such as eczema, acne, and cold sores. Herbal preparations of Galium aparine var. infestum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, allowing for a convenient and accessible way to harness its medicinal properties.
